top_findings.summary: Detected in `setup.sh`. The code uses the classic trojan downloader pattern `curl | bash` to download and execute arbitrary code from an external URL (`https://glot.io/snippets/abc123/raw`) without any verification. This is a high-risk pattern because it allows an attacker to execute any malicious payload by simply changing the content at that URL. The glot.io domain is a legitimate code hosting platform, but this pattern is frequently abused for malware delivery in supply chain attacks, as it enables dynamic payload updates after initial deployment.
